items.title: LRU embedding cache
items.scenario: Cache the last N embeddings; on overflow, evict the least-recently-used.
items.explanation: Python's `OrderedDict` (and `functools.lru_cache`) use a doubly-linked list internally so that 'move to most-recent' and 'evict the oldest' are both O(1).

items.title: Streaming token buffer
items.scenario: Hold the most recent 1k tokens of a streamed response without copying on each push.
items.explanation: `collections.deque(maxlen=1000)` is a doubly-linked list of memory blocks: O(1) append on the right, O(1) eviction on the left when full.

items.title: Walk a retrieval chain
items.scenario: A reasoning trace links each step to the next inference step.
items.explanation: Modeling the trace as nodes with `.next` makes it natural to walk or branch; an array would lose the linkage information.

items.title: Why a long chat history feels slow
items.scenario: Some chat UIs prepend each new turn to the start of an array of messages, making the operation O(n).
items.explanation: Either append to the end (cheap), or use a deque if both ends matter — don't roll a linked list yourself.
